mechanism_narrative: Monthly acceleration in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utilities output, which raises aggregate corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following months with positive INDPRO changes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5% signals tightening labor-market slack and stronger wage income growth, which boosts consumption forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 5.5% and declining over prior 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below 5.5% and declining, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.25% while the consumer price index rises more than 0.3% over three months, deeply negative real rates force investors out of cash and into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 3-month change exceeds 0.3%
failure_condition: Joint signal instances fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,200 thousand units in a post-recession recovery phase signals durable construction demand and spillover consumption, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1200 during post-recession recovery periods, S&P 500 60-day returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 600 thousand persons signals labor demand acceleration that outpaces consensus expectations, causing a rapid upward repricing of the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following 3-month PAYEMS changes above 600 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts capture broad-based consumer demand that single indicators miss, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases month-over-month and HOUST increases month-over-month
failure_condition: Instances where both RSAFS and HOUST increase month-over-month produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year window, or the interaction term is insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% signals extreme monetary accommodation that compresses overnight risk-free rates and front-loads equity risk premia, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) versus the unconditional mean over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in industrial production signals contracting capacity utilization and falling manufacturing profitability, which forces negative earnings revisions and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following negative 3-month INDPRO changes are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
